title: spergularia villosa var. rupestris
intro: Spergularia villosa var. rupestris, also known as hairy sand-spurrey or hairy spurrey, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Spergularia villosa var. rupestris include potential applications in wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use, although its preparation and consumption should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
